a:r.a:t: TAM Composition | Depot ship repair and nuclear and complex overhauls drove ~74% of the $
FY2025 MRO TAM, led by DDG availabilities and SSN / CVN overhauls
DDG 25% ($1.2B) and LPD 13% ($613M) lead; T-AO, CVN, LSD, LCS each 6-7%. Availabilities at SWRMC and MARMC.
SSN 66% ($1,255M) and CVN 18% ($337M) drive the segment; DDG and LCS trace. Submarine refueling overhauls at GDEB; CVN RCOH at HII Newport News.
DDG 6%; LCS, LPD, T-AO, CVN at 2-3% each. Propulsion, pumps, valves, HVAC, ship structural systems.
SSBN 55% ($319M). Trident II and VLS sustainment on Ohio-class SSBNs.
T-AO, DDG, T-AKE, CVN each 8-10%; ESB 4%. Husbanding, QC and testing, OEM tech reps.
SSBN 13% ($42M) Trident II support; CVN, SSN, DDG trace.

Note: (Uncl) columns on HM&E, Electronics, and Combat Systems reflect equipment-maintenance contracts that reference components rather than specific hulls. Nuclear & Complex Overhauls covers SSN, CVN, and surface combatants, with ~10% residual unclassified. Source: FPDS FY2025 contract obligations, U.S. Navy and U.S. Coast Guard. Data as of April 2026.
FY2025 MRO Bottom-up TAM Work Segment x Vessel Type ($M)
